name: File Parser Memory Corruption and Exploit Development
description: Attack tree for turning a document or media parser weakness into a reliable memory-corruption exploit path with delivery, triage, and primitive analysis.
focus_areas: File-format grammar recovery and parser reachability
Malicious file delivery and environment-specific exploitation
prompt_hints: Prefer parser states, corpus minimization, sanitizer output, heap shaping, and exploit primitive language over generic compromise steps.
Extend the tree into repro, exploitability, mitigations, and detection ideas tied to file parsing internals.
root_objective: Turn a reachable file parser flaw into a reproducible exploitation path for offensive planning

nodes.title: Exploit File Parser Weakness for Code Execution or Sensitive Data Access
nodes.description: Reverse engineer the target file parser, reproduce the vulnerable state, and turn the bug into a usable exploit path or trust-boundary bypass.
nodes.platform: Desktop client or server-side file parser
nodes.attack_surface: Document, media, archive, or custom file ingestion path
nodes.required_tools: Ghidra, WinDbg, ASan, PageHeap, corpus tooling

nodes.title: Map Parser Trust Boundary and Reachable Grammar
nodes.description: Identify how attacker-controlled file content crosses trust boundaries and which parsing stages are reachable before validation or sandboxing.
nodes.attack_surface: File loading, preview, import, or indexing path

nodes.title: Recover File Format Grammar and Parser Entry Points
nodes.description: Trace file-signature checks, chunk handlers, object deserializers, and decompression stages to recover the grammar the attacker can manipulate.
nodes.attack_surface: Magic bytes, object headers, chunk tables
nodes.required_tools: Ghidra, Binary Ninja, custom format scripts

nodes.title: Identify Unsafe Object Lifetimes or Bounds Logic
nodes.description: Pinpoint copy loops, integer truncations, allocator assumptions, reference-count bugs, or state-machine transitions where malformed input can corrupt memory or logic.
nodes.attack_surface: Chunk parsing, deserialization, decompression, metadata handling

nodes.title: Build Reproduction and Crash-Triage Workflow
nodes.description: Create a stable harness and evidence trail that reproduces the vulnerable condition with minimal unrelated application noise.

nodes.title: Construct Minimal Malicious File or Corpus Seed
nodes.description: Iteratively minimize the triggering sample so only the grammar elements needed to hit the target code path remain, making repro and fuzzing faster.

nodes.title: Instrument Target with Crash and Coverage Telemetry
nodes.description: Attach sanitizers, PageHeap, ETW, or debugger scripting so faulting state, heap layout, and branch coverage are available during triage.

nodes.title: Derive Exploit Primitive from Crash Behaviour
nodes.description: Turn the reproduced fault into a concrete primitive such as controlled read, controlled write, vtable corruption, auth bypass, or data exfiltration path.

nodes.title: Classify Bug as Overflow, UAF, Type Confusion, or Logic Corruption
nodes.description: Inspect the faulting object state and memory ownership assumptions to determine the real primitive instead of stopping at a generic crash label.
nodes.attack_surface: Heap, stack, object lifetime, or parser state machine

nodes.title: Shape Heap or Object Graph for Reliable Control
nodes.description: Manipulate allocation order, file structure, and parser state to stabilize corruption into a repeatable control primitive or sensitive-data disclosure.
nodes.attack_surface: Heap layout and parser object graph
nodes.required_tools: Heap grooming scripts, custom corpus generator

nodes.title: Operationalize Delivery and Environment Constraints
nodes.description: Connect the parser exploit path to the real delivery vector, victim workflow, and mitigations present in production targets.
nodes.attack_surface: Email attachment, download, sync, or server-side processing pipeline

nodes.title: Package Malicious File for Real Delivery Workflow
nodes.description: Adapt the proof of concept to the real file-delivery channel, accounting for preview handlers, archive wrappers, scanning, content normalization, and user-open behavior.
nodes.attack_surface: Attachment, download, file sync, or import pipeline
nodes.required_tools: Custom packer, phishing or upload tooling
